title: The Life we have is very great. by Emily Dickinson — Deep Poetry Lines
content:
The Life we have is very great.
The Life that we shall see
Surpasses it, we know, because
It is Infinity.
But when all Space has been beheld
And all Dominion shown
The smallest Human Heart's extent
Reduces it to none.
meaning: "The Life we have is very great." by Emily Dickinson is a deep poem consisting of 8 lines. The poet explores themes of deep through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "The Life we have is very great. The Life that we shall see...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Emily Dickinson's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
